task_completion.description: The agent successfully loads the brain MRI scan into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer application window is visible with the T1-contrast brain MRI loaded in the slice viewers (Red, Yellow, Green windows).

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to an axial slice showing the maximum cross-section of the tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial slice view (usually the Red viewer) displays a clear, large, hyperintense (bright) tumor region, distinct from the surrounding brain tissue.

task_completion.description: The agent places the first ruler line (D1) across the longest axis of the tumor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler line (Markups tool) is drawn spanning the longest dimension of the visible tumor on the slice.

task_completion.description: The agent places the second ruler line (D2) perpendicular to the first line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second ruler line is drawn across the tumor. It must appear visually perpendicular (approx. 90 degrees) to the first line (D1) and span the tumor width.

task_completion.description: The measurement values for both lines are visible in the interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module table or the view overlay displays the length values (in mm) for both placed lines.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the measurement markups to the specified file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog or a file browser window shows 'who_measurements.mrk.json' being saved or present in the '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent generates and saves the JSON report with the calculated product.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal output, or file browser shows the content or creation of 'who_report.json', containing fields for diameters and the bidimensional product.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI tools (Markups, Slice navigation) to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the toolbar, slice views, or Markups module, rather than the task being completed solely via terminal scripts without visual verification.

integrity.description: The agent calculates the product based on its own measurements, not external data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open a text file containing pre-existing answers or copy-paste values from the task description/prompt.

integrity.description: The measurements are derived from actual interaction with the image data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the progressive placement of ruler lines on the tumor, confirming the agent visually identified the measurement points.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The maximum diameter error tolerance for tumor measurement is 5.0 mm. Measurements should be visually consistent with the tumor boundaries shown in the scan.
